evals.prompt: hey can you refactor this process_order function? it's way too long and doing too many things. the file is at evals/scenario1_long_function.py
evals.expected_output: Should analyze first, present HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW priorities, ask for approval BEFORE making any changes, then refactor ONE thing at a time with verification between each step

evals.prompt: I need to add OAuth2 support (Google login) to this auth module, and while you're at it clean up the code - the login method is getting messy. File is evals/scenario2_refactor_plus_feature.py
evals.expected_output: Should explicitly separate refactoring from feature work. Either refactor first then add OAuth2, or add OAuth2 first then refactor. Never mix both in the same transformation

evals.prompt: this csv parser code is really ugly - single letter variable names, no docstrings, hard to read. can you clean it up? file is evals/scenario3_stable_ugly.py
evals.expected_output: Should push back on refactoring. The file header says it's stable (last modified 2022, 0 PRs in 12 months, 100% test coverage). Should explain that ugly but stable code doesn't need refactoring and suggest leaving it alone
